= Add `flexCorrect` to capabilities
:navtitle: Add `flexCorrect`

Use `flexCorrect` to select the right element across different devices and when layouts change between runs. This reduces `Element not found` errors during testing.

`flexCorrect` supports two Xium automation script types:

* A Xium automation script xref:automation-testing:scripting/auto-generate-an-appium-script.adoc[generated by Kobiton AI] from a manual session.
* A custom (non-generated) Xium automation script. This type requires a baseline automation session before `flexCorrect` can run. See <<_prepare_a_baseline_session>>.

[#_prepare_a_baseline_session]
== Prepare a baseline session

Skip this section for Xium scripts generated by Kobiton AI.

For a custom Xium automation script, create a baseline session first:

. Run the script once on a Kobiton device without `kobiton:flexCorrect` enabled. Kobiton creates an automation session from this run.
. Note the session ID. Use it as the value for `kobiton:baselineSessionId` when you configure capabilities below.

== Locate config file

Open the config file in your test suite. The name and location vary by framework, but the file is typically here:

.Example
[source,plaintext]
----
root
└── src
  └── test
      └── Config # Java TestNG
      └── Config.java # Java JUnit
      └── config.js # NodeJS Mocha
----

== Add capability

In the config file, locate `DesiredCapabilities`, and add the following lines:

[cols="2,3", options="header"]
|===
| Capability
| Value

| `kobiton:flexCorrect`
| `true`

| `kobiton:baselineSessionId`
| Select a baseline session by assigning its xref:automation-testing:get-a-session-id/using-the-kobiton-api.adoc[session ID]. For a custom Xium script, use the session ID from
<<_prepare_a_baseline_session>>.

| `kobiton:scriptlessEnabled` (optional) [.small]#_Needs confirmation_#
| `true`. Improves element-matching accuracy for custom Xium scripts.
|===

.Example
[source,java]
----
public static DesiredCapabilities getIPhone11ProIOS146DesiredCapabilities() {
      DesiredCapabilities capabilities = new DesiredCapabilities();

      // Add these capabilities:

      capabilities.setCapability("kobiton:flexCorrect", true);  // Enable flexCorrect by setting capability to true.
      capabilities.setCapability("kobiton:baselineSessionId", 0000011); //  Select a baseline session by assigning its session ID.

  return capabilities;
}
----

Save your changes. During execution, if an element's layout changes, `flexCorrect` uses AI to locate the element and continue the test instead of returning `Element not found`.

[NOTE]
After a session, you can check xref:session-explorer:manage-sessions.adoc[how many elements were corrected].

Open questions or verification needed

1. scriptlessEnabled capability — Needs confirmation.
The ticket lists the optional capability as "scriptlessenabled:true": true. That string is malformed (a literal :true is embedded in the key), and no scriptlessenabled / scriptlessEnabled capability is documented in capabilities/available-capabilities.adoc. Before publish, confirm with the KOB-50780 / KOB-50495 owner:
- Exact capability name and casing (best guess: kobiton:scriptlessEnabled)
- Whether it should be added as a first-class entry in available-capabilities.adoc
- Whether it is truly optional or required for the custom-script path
2. xref path verification.
The draft uses xref:automation-testing:scripting/auto-generate-an-appium-script.adoc[]. The ticket references the URL docs.kobiton.com/automation-testing/scripting/auto-generate-an-appium-script —
confirm the AsciiDoc file lives at the expected module path before publishing.
3. Should this update also touch available-capabilities.adoc?
If kobiton:scriptlessEnabled is confirmed as a real capability, a matching entry should be added there. Out of scope for this KOB-50780 update unless you want me to expand.
4. Intro phrasing change.
I removed the existing "Learn how to add flexCorrect..." opening because it violates the style guide ("Avoid 'Learn how to...' intros"). Confirm this scope expansion is acceptable, or revert to the original opening if you'd rather keep KOB-50780 minimal and let Style Sheriff catch it separately.
